headline: Survey severely off-track: 8 responses against 1,350 target represents 0.6% completion.
collection_status: Data collection is critically behind pace. At current rate (8 responses from 2 regions), reaching 1,350 responses across 10 regions by target is unachievable without immediate intervention. Only MARA and SONGWE regions active; 8 regions have zero engagement.
key_findings: Respondent base skews heavily toward volunteers (75%) over regional coordinators (25%), limiting institutional perspective needed for scale readiness assessment
Top priorities cluster around expansion (new_programs: 5, expand_kf: 3) and government partnership integration, indicating stakeholder appetite exists but capacity/policy alignment unclear
Scale readiness average of 3.0/10 (assumed scale) suggests significant gaps in systems, digital monitoring, and inter-stakeholder communication for expansion
Feedback reveals consistent requests for teacher incentives, program monitoring systems, and government policy alignment—actionable but requires sustained partner coordination
flags: Geographic coverage critically narrow: 2 of 10 regions active. Risk of regional bias invalidating survey findings for national scale recommendations.
Sampling composition threatens validity: 75% volunteer responses may overweight grassroots optimism; RC underrepresentation (2 responses) limits leadership perspective on feasibility.
Collection velocity unsustainable: 8 responses in unspecified timeframe. If this represents >1 month of effort, target completion is implausible without resource reallocation.
recommendation: Immediately audit collection methodology and resource allocation across all 10 regions. Establish weekly response targets per region and enforce RC quota (minimum 30% of regional sample) to ensure geographic and institutional diversity. Redeploy field staff or incentivize completion if current pace persists beyond week 2.
